title: XServer Business Review: Shared, Virtual, and Physical Managed Servers
metaTitle: XServer Business Review: Shared, Virtual, and Physical Managed Servers
description: Review XServer Business shared hosting and managed virtual and physical dedicated servers by pricing, SLA, controls, and support.
lead: XServer Business extends the XServer operating model with business administration, setup assistance, security controls, and managed dedicated options. The choice among shared, virtual, and physical plans should follow isolation, access control, operational support, and budget rather than company status alone.
author: Luqevora Editorial Team

sections.heading: Verdict: evaluate when business operations or isolation justify it
body: Start by testing whether shared XServer Business meets a normal company-site and mail requirement. Evaluate virtual or physical managed dedicated plans when load, separation, security, permissions, or limited in-house administration justify them.
Business-oriented support does not replace internal access control, content maintenance, application security, or third-party service resilience.

sections.heading: Affiliate conditions
body: The A8.net program lists JPY 10,000 for shared hosting, JPY 20,000 for managed virtual dedicated, and JPY 30,000 for managed physical dedicated on contracts of at least six months. Recommendations must be based on isolation and operating requirements, not commission size.
Use the XServer Business material only on business-service pages, not the regular XServer destination.

sections.heading: Pricing and service structure
body: XServer Business is divided into shared hosting, managed virtual dedicated servers, and managed physical dedicated servers. Shared hosting offers Standard, Premium, and Enterprise tiers, with lower monthly equivalents on longer contracts. Setup fees mean it should not be compared with regular XServer on monthly price alone.

Virtual managed dedicated plans balance isolation and cost, while physical plans assign a physical server to one customer. Evaluate normal rates, setup charges, contract length, migration work, and internal operating cost instead of a temporary campaign figure.

rows: From JPY 990 monthly equivalent on 36 months
Shared environment and standard control panel
Company sites, email, and assisted operations
From JPY 3,762 per month on 36 months plus setup
Higher load, separation, limited in-house administration
From JPY 19,800 per month on 36 months plus setup
From JPY 29,700 per month plus setup

sections.heading: Business operating requirements
body: Companies should review separated administrator rights, access removal after staff changes, incident notification, invoices, logs, backups, WAF, tamper detection, and mail authentication. Capabilities differ across shared and dedicated XServer Business plans.

Assisted configuration and migration do not automatically cover application warranties, third-party SaaS, DNS ownership, mail delivery, or software maintenance. Document the provider and customer responsibility boundary before signing.

sections.heading: Choosing shared, virtual, or physical
body: Shared hosting is the cost-oriented option for common company sites and email. Managed virtual dedicated hosting is relevant when stronger separation and provider-managed operations are needed. Physical dedicated hosting fits explicit physical-isolation or exclusive-resource requirements.

A dedicated environment does not remove application failures, configuration mistakes, compromised accounts, or external API outages. Define availability, recovery time, backup location, monitoring, and named owners.

sections.heading: Pre-contract checklist
body: Use the trial to inspect administration, permissions, email, SSL, backups, support channels, invoicing, and migration. The affiliate condition requires a contract of at least six months, but editorial guidance should prioritize the reader’s actual operating term.
bullets: Calculate setup and full contract cost
Assign internal incident and account owners
Test mail and recovery during the trial

faqs.question: Is XServer Business the same as regular XServer?
faqs.answer: No. Regular XServer has a plan named Business, while XServer Business is a separate service offering shared and managed virtual and physical dedicated hosting.

faqs.question: Does every company need XServer Business?
faqs.answer: No. A smaller company site may fit regular XServer. Decide by permissions, assisted operations, isolation, dedicated resources, and service-level requirements.

faqs.question: Is a trial available?
faqs.answer: The official XServer Business site advertises a fourteen-day trial. Confirm eligibility, payment, migration, and dedicated-plan conditions at application.
